one_time_description: Transfer of Mount Currie Chevron gas station assets to Lil'wat Retail Operations Limited Partnership at fair value of $3,759,278
economic_notes: Nation has investments in multiple limited partnerships including Lil'wat Holdings LP, Lil'wat Forestry Ventures LP, Lil'wat Construction Enterprises LP, Lil'wat Retail Operations LP, Lil'wat Management Services LP. Whistler Blackcomb Master Development Agreement revenues. BC Hydro settlement funds. Baxter Creek Developments proceeds. BC First Nations Gaming Revenue Sharing. Squamish Mills purchase in year.
notes: Fiscal year 2021 (ended March 31, 2021). Nation uses fund accounting with Operations Fund, Capital Fund, Xet'olacw Housing Fund, and Xet'olacw Community School Fund. Business enterprises (Lil'wat Holdings LP and others) are accounted for on modified equity basis - not fully consolidated. Transfer of Chevron gas station assets ($3,759,278) to Lil'wat Retail Operations LP is a significant non-recurring item. No schedule of remuneration provided in extracted text. total_government_transfers estimated from ISC + CMHC + DFO + FNESC + FNHA + BC Gaming + Province of BC lines. osr calculated as total revenue minus government transfers. Expenses by program from fund-level breakdown rather than functional segment detail. Segmented information table was partially cut off in source document.
